He was part of the MI squad in 2020 and the Kolkata Knight Riders in 2024, but did not feature in the playing XI during those seasons. Rutherford has played 23 IPL matches so far, having earlier represented Delhi Capitals in 2019 and Royal Challengers Bengaluru in 2022. Acquired by GT for a fee of INR 2.6 crore, Rutherford will move to MI at his existing fee. SRH will play their first game on the opening night of the 2026 season as they take on defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) on Saturday, March 28th, in Bengaluru. Kishan celebrated his SRH debut by smashing 106* off just 47 deliveries in the team’s 2025 season opener against Rajasthan Royals (RR). The right-arm pacer, who is still recovering from an injury, is set to miss the first few games of the season. Abhishek Sharma has been appointed as the vice-captain while Kishan leads the side. Kishan, who recently played a huge role in India’s historic ICC Men’s T20 World Cup 2026 winning campaign, joined SRH after being acquired for INR 11.25 Crore in the Mega Auction ahead of the 2025 season. title: Rajat Patidar labels RCB’s collective brilliance as ‘positive’ sign for IPL 2026
raw_text: Download Now Submitted by Amith Chakrapani on Sun, 03/29/2026 - 13:53 Rajat Patidar labels RCB’s collective brilliance as ‘positive’ sign for IPL 2026 29 Mar, 2026 Editor Rajat Patidar labels RCB’s collective brilliance as ‘positive’ sign for IPL 2026 29 Mar, 2026 By Editor The Royal Challengers Bengaluru stamped their authority over Sunrisers Hyderabad with an emphatic win in the IPL 2026 season opener. Rajat Patidar, the RCB captain walked in and started belting out the shots for fun. I’ve seen him take many catches in the air, so that was a brilliant effort.” NEXT This was a hell of a way to start the campaign – Jacob Duffy on his match-winning spell against SRH 29 Mar RCB TV TRENDING RCB's Auction Planning Videos, Final Episode: Overseas Pacer Discussion | Jacob Duffy Signing 23 Mar RCB's first Warm Up Game: A Run-fest at the Chinnaswamy | IPL 2026 | Bold Diaries 23 Mar RCB's Auction Planning Videos, Episode 3: Indian Batter | IPL 2026 22 Mar RCB Auction Planning Videos Episode 2: Indian Pace Bowler | IPL 2026 21 Mar RCB's Auction Planning Videos: Episode 1 | IPL 2026 | Bold Diaries 20 Mar Footer menu Home About Us News Fixtures PRIVACY AND COOKIE NOTICE Rcb Tv Team Terms & Conditions Shop T&C Photos Shop Contact Us Connect with RCB Players on the RCB App. --> Rajat Patidar delighted with RCB’s collective performance against SRH in IPL 2026 opener Skip to main content Download Our App       #Playbold 0 Download Our App Enter your keywords Test #Playbold Main navigation Home News Rcb Tv Team Fixtures RCB BAR & CAFE TICKETS Shop More DIGITAL DAY Sports Report Go Green Photos About Us Privacy Policy Terms & Conditions Shop T&C CSR POLICY Annual Return FY2021-2022 Annual Return FY2022-2023 Annual Return FY2023-2024 Contact Us Connect with RCB Players on the RCB App. I think there are no words for him.” Patidar’s admiration for Virat Kohli’s incredible knock was simply more palpable as he said, “Every time he (Kohli) goes in, especially when the team is chasing, he delivers as the number one batter. The way he took the wickets of Head and Abhishek, I think that was the turning point for us.” Underlining RCB’s aggressive batting stance, Patidar said, “Not a policy, but we are assessing the wicket. The way he played and stayed till the end was outstanding.” He concluded by talking about Phil Salt’s electric take of Ishan Kishan, adding, “It was a terrific catch by Phil Salt. Talking at the post-match presentation, Rajat opened up about how the collective brilliance helped RCB shape this massive win. The way Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal played their innings, I think that laid a solid base for the other batsmen. Download Now Copyright Royal Challengers Sports Pvt.

Had it not been for a slight slowdown in the middle after Rajat Patidar and Jitesh Sharma fell on successive deliveries, it could have been a quicker chase. Despite midway challenges, Virat Kohli’s steady knock ensured RCB encountered no hiccups. If we finish with 25-26 balls to spare, we’ll also benefit in terms of net run rate.” Kohli concluded by adding more epithets to describe Padikkal’s innings, stating, “His knock was tremendous. Becomes Principal Partner of Lucknow Super Giants for Indian Premier League Season 19 Follow us on Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube LinkedIn IOS Android Login View Profile Logout Home Lucknow Super Giants Home Matches Standings Latest News & Features Photos Team IPL Auction 2026 Follow us on Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube LinkedIn IOS Android Home News Rishabh Pant returns as India gear up for South Africa tour at home Rishabh Pant returns as India brace for South Africa showdown 10 Nov, 2025 News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close After concluding a thrilling white ball series against Australia Down Under, the Indian men’s cricket team gears up for another mammoth challenge - a bilateral series against South Africa at home starting at Kolkata’s iconic Eden Gardens on November 14. After concluding a thrilling white ball series against Australia Down Under, the Indian men’s cricket team gears up for another mammoth challenge - a bilateral series against South Africa at home starting at Kolkata’s iconic Eden Gardens on November 14.The long-awaited series will comprise of two Tests, three ODIs and five T20 Internationals, filled with gripping action between two dynamic teams of the cricketing world.The return of Rishabh PantThe biggest talking point heading into the series is the return of India’s star wicketkeeper-batter, Rishabh Pant, to the Test squad. The flamboyant southpaw, who serves as India’s Test vice-captain, is set to reunite with the whites after recovering from a toe injury that sidelined him during the Anderson-Tendulkar Trophy.Pant recently made his on-field comeback in style - leading the India A side against South Africa A in a two-match unofficial Test series and steering them to a win in the opener. His presence behind the stumps and his fearless approach with the bat are expected to be central to India’s success this time around.Known for his attacking mindset and ability to turn matches on their head, the Lucknow Super Giants skipper brings not just experience but an unshakable aura of confidence to the dressing room. title: Tommy Hilfiger Travel Gear announced as Official Travel Partner of Lucknow Super Giants for IPL 2026
raw_text: As the official travel partner, Tommy Hilfiger Travel Gear will equip the Lucknow Super Giants squad with a curated range of premium luggage and travel essentials designed to support the team through their rigorous travel schedule during the tournament.The collaboration underscores Tommy Hilfiger Travel Gear’s focus on blending style with functionality, ensuring that the players travel with comfort, durability, and iconic design, both on and off the field.Lucknow Super Giants, known for their spirited gameplay and rapidly growing fan base, continue to strengthen their off-field partnerships with brands that resonate with their ethos of excellence and forward-thinking.This association is set to elevate the travel experience of the team while reinforcing Tommy Hilfiger Travel Gear’s presence in India’s high-energy sporting ecosystem during one of the country’s most celebrated sporting seasons. title: Introducing the Punjab Kings Squad for the 2026 IPL Season
raw_text: He was also secured at his base price of INR 30 lakh, adding depth and future potential to the team.The complete squad: Priyansh Arya, Prabhsimran Singh, Shreyas Iyer, Nehal Wadhera, Shashank Singh, Marcus Stoinis, Azmatullah Omarzai, Vijaykumar Vyshak, Arshdeep Singh, Yuzvendra Chahal, Harpreet Brar, Suryansh Shedge, Vishnu Vinod, Xavier Bartlett, Yash Thakur, Mitchell Owen, Harnoor Singh, Musheer Khan, Pyla Avinash, Marco Jansen, and Lockie Ferguson, Cooper Connolly, Ben Dwarshuis, Pravin Dubey, Vishal Nishad. Armed with ₹11.5 crore and a stable 21-player core, Punjab Kings focus on targeted upgrades at the IPL 2026 auction Armed with ₹11.5 crore and a stable 21-player core, Punjab Kings focus on targeted upgrades at the IPL 2026 auction With the IPL 2026 auction set to take place in Abu Dhabi, Punjab Kings enter the big day with clarity, balance, and a sharply defined plan. With four total slots left, including two overseas spots, PBKS have both the structure and flexibility to make targeted, high-impact moves.The retained list reflects a blend of experience, youth, and all-round depth. Overseas recruits Xavier Bartlett, Azmatullah Omarzai, and Mitchell Owen add variety to the pace and all-round options, giving Punjab a well-rounded squad even before entering the auction hall. With a largely complete roster already in place, the focus for Punjab Kings at the auction will be on specific, fine-tuning acquisitions rather than wholesale changes. With limited but sufficient purse, PBKS are expected to adopt a calculated approach, targeting players who fit specific roles rather than engaging in bidding wars. With four total slots left, including two overseas spots, PBKS have both the structure and flexibility to make targeted, high-impact moves. As the auction in Abu Dhabi approaches, Punjab Kings appear well-prepared and strategically aligned. The franchise has also placed faith in rising domestic talents such as Prabhsimran Singh, Priyansh Arya, Harnoor Singh, Nehal Wadhera, Musheer Khan, Pyla Avinash and Suryansh Shedge, ensuring continuity in its long-term development strategy. Additionally, the Kings will look to bolster their local pace and spin reserves by adding Indian bowling options, a key component in navigating long tournaments and varied conditions. Their strong retained core allows them to prioritize tactical additions that enhance squad balance while maintaining the flexibility to adapt across venues and oppositions. A reliable backup wicketkeeper is also on the agenda, ensuring depth behind Prabhsimran Singh, Vishnu Vinod and maintaining balance in case of injuries or tactical shifts.

Rajasthan Royals has always been a socially conscious franchise, funding impactful initiatives since 2013, including cleft lip surgeries, girl child education, elderly care, grassroots cricket development, and raising awareness on early cancer detection in collaboration with the Government of Rajasthan and Tata Trusts, among others With the establishment of the Rajasthan Royals Foundation in 2019, we strive to amplify our impact by fostering partnerships and implementing large-scale behaviour change methodologies, all with the overarching goal of enabling empowered women across Rajasthan. 13:42 I’m not coming back underprepared – Virat Kohli on how he readied himself for IPL 2026 29 Mar, 2026 Editor I’m not coming back underprepared – Virat Kohli on how he readied himself for IPL 2026 29 Mar, 2026 By Editor Virat Kohli returned to action in IPL 2026, taking the field for RCB for a record 19th season. But the former RCB captain once again proved to the world that he is hungrier than ever. The highest run-scorer for RCB last season started the campaign with similar truculence as RCB made an absolute mockery of what was a formidable 200-plus total on the board. Talking at the post-match presentation, Virat Kohli opened up about being part of T20 cricket after almost a year, stating, “Thanks for such lovely words. One of the major talking points ahead of the game was definitely how well Virat Kohli would perform when he took center stage. Returning to T20s after almost a year, there were obvious speculations about his freewheeling scoring. And as long as you’re physically fit and mentally excited, both those things come together nicely. Download Now Copyright Royal Challengers Sports Pvt.

Dhoni, who turns 45 this year, is one among four wicket-keepers in the CSK squad, alongside Sanju Samson (who was traded from Rajasthan Royals), Kartik Sharma, and Urvil Patel. A statement from Chennai Super Kings on the morning of the season opener confirmed that Dhoni is undergoing rehabilitation for a calf injury, likely leaving the team without their star player for the early part of the tournament. Chennai Super Kings' first game is against Rajasthan Royals on March 30 in Guwahati. In the first two weeks, the period Dhoni is likely to take to recover, CSK face Punjab Kings (home), Royal Challengers Bengaluru (away) and Delhi Capitals (home), apart from their season opener against Rajasthan Royals. It has also been learnt that Sam Curran, who was traded to the Rajasthan Royals from CSK, is also set to be ruled out of IPL 2026 with a groin injury. The Super Kings confirmed to Cricbuzz that they are weighing their replacement options from the Registered Available Player Pool (RAPP) , which contains over 1,300 players. The franchise named Ishan Kishan their captain for the opening few matches of the tournament, which Cummins has been confirmed to miss. This is the second injury blow dealt to the Sunrisers, who are also sweating over the availability of regular skipper Pat Cummins. While the franchise confirmed Edwards' unavailability to Cricbuzz, the specifics of his injury are unknown at this point. It remains to be seen if the Royals and the Sunrisers also explore replacement options from the RAPP. Elsewhere, Ellis' countryman Jack Edwards has also been ruled out of the tournament with an injury. © Getty CSK's Ellis has picked up a hamstring injury. While it is not known as to when or how he picked up the injury, Ellis did struggle with a hamstring niggle that kept him out of the knockout stage of the Big Bash League (BBL) where he led the Hobart Hurricanes. Ellis was expected to lead CSK's death-bowling charge, with the five-time champions having let go of Matheesha Pathirana ahead of the player auction in December. Curran was part of England's T20 World Cup squad which made the semifinal. High-value seeded injury article for CSK. Also contains replacement context relevant to RR vs CSK planning.

Download App Play Store App Store WhatsApp Riyan Parag's maturity, clarity of thought make him the right person to lead the Royals, says Kumar Sangakkara The Royals head coach and Director of Cricket also explained why Parag stood out even within a batch of worthy candidates for the role. After a brief stint as the team’s stand-in skipper last season, Parag was named the Royals’ full-time captain for the upcoming season, replacing Sanju Samson. "There were five candidates, including Sandeep Sharma and Ravindra Jadeja ,” Sangakkara revealed. “We had very strict parameters as to how we are going to choose, what is the process, so we had lots of conversations with all candidates and we realised all are very capable of captaining our side.” “Riyan came across as the top candidate because in all his conversations, he seemed to have matured a lot,” he noted. We went with an open mind and gave due respect to all the players, gave all an equal chance and picked the best candidate." Sangakkara, an accomplished captain during his playing days who led Sri Lanka to the final of the 2011 ODI World Cup, also underlined the fine distinction between captaincy and leadership. On the eve of the Royals’ IPL 2026 opener against Chennai Super Kings at the ACA Stadium in Guwahati , Sangakkara revealed that the Royals undertook an extensive and structured evaluation process before settling on Parag for the all-important role. Some of those questions were quite uncomfortable for all of them.” “Riyan, with his maturity, his vision, wanted not just to captain but to lead the team, it made him the leading candidate. He also emphasised that Parag will never be short of guidance, with a wealth of experience and cricketing intelligence within the squad to draw upon whenever required. The Royals’ think tank didn’t just assess tactical acumen but placed significant emphasis on personality, self-awareness and long-term vision to think for the team.

Download App Play Store App Store WhatsApp Rajasthan Royals captains: From Shane Warne to Riyan Parag - full list A total of seven captains have led the team since 2008, helping Rajasthan Royals win a title and qualifying for the knock-outs six times. The decision was made as Sanju Samson was still recovering from an injury and despite being deemed fit enough to bat, the Royals' full-time captain was not cleared to perform his usual wicket-keeping or fielding duties. Riyan Parag Riyan Parag was appointed captain for the Rajasthan Royals for their opening three IPL 2025 matches against Sunrisers Hyderabad, Kolkata Knight Riders and Chennai Super Kings. Riyan Parag was named the full-time captain of the Rajasthan Royals ahead of the IPL 2026 season. Shane Warne Shane Warne, Rajasthan Royals’ first captain , laid the blueprint for what would turn out to be the team’s vision when he led a bunch of young, promising and inexperienced domestic players to capture ultimate glory by winning the inaugural IPL. When Parag, 23, led out the Royals against SRH for their season opener in Hyderabad on March 23, he became the youngest Rajasthan Royals captain and the fifth-youngest in IPL history. Starting with the title-winning Shane Warne in 2008 and standing currently with Riyan Parag , the captaincy baton at the Royals has passed through many able hands. Rajasthan Royals went with Shane Warne as captain in IPL 2008, for his experience at the international level, though he was never a regular captain for Australia. In 2023, the Royals finished fifth in the table under Sanju, missing qualification to the knock-outs by a whisker. He held the record for most wins as a Rajasthan Royals captain before Sanju Samson took the top spot in IPL 2025. Rahul Dravid was Rajasthan Royals’ oldest captain when he took over the reins at the age of 39 in IPL 2012. Rahul Dravid has the best record among Rajasthan Royals’ captains when it comes to win percentage. Dravid finished his first season as captain of Rajasthan Royals in seventh place in IPL 2012. Ajinkya Rahane Ajinkya Rahane was announced as Rajasthan Royals captain for IPL 2018. He is Rajasthan Royals' most successful captain in the IPL. Here's the full Rajasthan Royals captain list . In 2024, he went past Shane Warne's record of leading the Rajasthan Royals in the most number of matches and became the most successful skipper in terms of number of wins in 2025. 30 Mar, 2026 Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Rajasthan Royals has had a roller coaster ride in the Indian Premier League so far. It means we are trying to create a successful environment for one another.” Watson captained the Royals for two seasons, covering 21 matches and made the play-offs in IPL 2015. The Mumbaikar did well in his first season as skipper to help Rajasthan Royals finish fourth, but lost in the play-offs to Kolkata Knight Riders. Rahul Dravid Taking over the reins from Shane Warne and carrying forward Rajasthan Royals’ culture was a challenge in itself. Sanju Samson Sanju Samson joined the list of illustrious names to lead Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, at 26. Warne remained captain of the Royals from 2009 to 2011 and left his legacy with the team. Shane Watson Shane Watson led Rajasthan Royals from IPL 2014 once Rahul Dravid retired. In IPL 2022, Sanju was able to steer Rajasthan Royals to their first final since 2008. Samson was also the only Royals captain to have led the team into two playoffs in IPL. Parag won two and lost six matches as Rajasthan Royals' skipper in IPL 2025. Smith was 24 when he captained the Royals for the first time. His first full season as captain for the Royals was in 2020. Riyan Parag is the current captain of the team. “He led the team to the playoffs in 2018 in a very challenging scenario and that will go down as one of the great comebacks of the IPL.” Steve Smith Steve Smith filled in as captain of Rajasthan, first for Shane Watson in seasons 2014 and 2015 before replacing Rahane midway through IPL 2019. Having spent an entire season under Warne’s captaincy, Dravid was able to take the team forward in the same direction, with an emphasis on creating a supportive environment for youngsters to prosper in the IPL. The Royals continued their good run in IPL 2013 into the Champions League tournament later that year, when Rahul Dravid led them to the final, again falling short to Mumbai Indians in the final hurdle. Ravindra Jadeja, Sohail Tanvir, Ajinkya Rahane and Naman Ojha all bloomed under his tutelage to become better cricketers for their country and states. He could captain Rajasthan only for 27 games and won 15 of them and is second only to Rahul Dravid in terms of win percentage. The Royals reached their second playoffs under Samson in IPL 2024, finishing third in the points table with 17 points. But Rahul Dravid, with his experience and leadership tenure with the Indian cricket team was the best fit. In IPL 2018, the Royals won four of their last five matches to make the playoffs.

The left-arm pacer, who has 6 IPL wickets to his name, has previously featured for GT, KKR, and Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB). Meanwhile, Khejroliya replaces the injured Prithviraj Yarra in GT. He will replace the injured Harshit and join KKR for INR 75 Lakh. Ishan Kishan and Abhishek Sharma , both entering the 2026 edition as T20 World champions , will once again form a vital cog in the batting order alongside the experienced Travis Head . Ishan Kishan , who made his SRH debut last year, seamlessly fitted into the explosive top order, amassing 354 runs at a strike rate in excess of 150 . David Payne also joins the squad for the 2026 edition, replacing Jack Edwards , who has been ruled out of the season due to injury. Abhishek Sharma scored 923 runs over the last two seasons at a strike rate nearing 200 , while Travis Head smashed 941 runs in the same period at a strike rate of 179 . SunRisers Hyderabad lead the head-to-head record against their opening-match opponents, having won 13 of the 25 encounters between the two teams. The power-packed Orange Army line-up will be led by Ishan Kishan , who takes charge in the absence of Pat Cummins . Heinrich Klaasen has been a consistent presence among the top run-getters since making his SRH debut in 2023. Joining this unit for the current edition is the big-hitting English star Liam Livingstone , who brings a wealth of T20 experience to the middle order, having scored over 7,500 runs in the format. Eshan Malinga returns for a second season, having picked up 13 wickets in seven matches last year. As the new season is upon us, once again, the squad is ready to Play With Fire .

Sahibzada Farhan stole the show with a sensational century, but it was his opening partner, Steve Smith, who did the early damage with a blistering 46 off just 20 balls, including two fours and four sixes. In fairness, the dew factor made life difficult for the bowlers and fielders, and Hyderabad Kingsmen were always on the back foot after failing to pick up a wicket in the powerplay. Masood and Minhas chipped in with handy cameos, while Farhan ensured he remained unbeaten till the very end to seal the win. The latter is: "Engaging in any conduct (whether through the use of language, gestures or otherwise) which is likely to offend, insult, humiliate, intimidate, threaten, disparage or vilify any reasonable person in the position of a Player, Team Official, Umpire, Match Referee or any other person (including a spectator) on the basis of their race, religion, culture, colour, descent, national or ethnic origin, sex, gender, sexual orientation, disability, marital status and/or maternity status." The sanctions for a breach will range from suspension for four matches to a ban for life, depending on the past record and its seriousness. The Anti-Discrimination Code is aimed at BCCI's "efforts to maintain the public image, popularity and integrity of the League by providing: (a) an effective means to deter any participant from conducting themselves in a manner that may be construed as racially and/or religiously offensive; and (b) a robust disciplinary procedure pursuant to which all matters of improper conduct of this nature in relation to the League can be dealt with fairly, with certainty and in an expeditious manner." There is, of course, a clear distinction between the Code of Conduct and the Anti-Discrimination Code. "All Players and Team Officials shall continue to be bound by and required to comply with the AntiDiscrimination Code until three (3) months after the termination of his or her contract or, in respect of any Team Official, any other arrangements with a Franchisee and BCCI shall continue to have jurisdiction over him/her under the Anti-Discrimination Code thereafter in respect of matters taking place prior to that point," the 11-page document, accessed by Cricbuzz, notes. Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) BCCI extends IPL Anti-Discrimination Code beyond season IPL 2026 | Cricbuzz.com BCCI extends IPL Anti-Discrimination Code beyond season The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has ruled that the IPL Anti-Discrimination Code will continue to apply to players, officials and stakeholders even after the conclusion of the season. But, the code clarifies, the Ombudsman "will have no jurisdiction to adjust, reverse or amend the results of any match." Any decision under the Anti-Discrimination Code shall be recognised and respected by the BCCI and the Franchisees automatically upon receipt of notice of the same, without the need for any further formality. In all the above cases, the mandatory requirement will be to "undergo a programme of education/counselling designed to promote the understanding and awareness of issues directly relevant to the offence that the Player or Team Official has been determined to have committed." The BCCI Ombudsman will adjudicate on the offence. Each of the BCCI and the Franchisees shall take all steps legally available to it to enforce and give effect to such decisions. It shall be a condition of participation in the League that all Franchisees shall comply with the Anti-Discrimination Code.

Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel won the toss and elected to bowl first, backing his bowlers to exploit the red-soil surface in Lucknow. However, Kuldeep Yadav (2 for 31) halted the charge by dismissing the dangerous Australian. When Axar Patel fell shortly after, the Capitals were staring down the barrel at 26 for 4 inside the Powerplay, giving LSG the record for their most wickets taken inside the first six overs in IPL history. Mohsin Khan (1 for 19) removed Nitish Rana (15 off 17), while young speedster Prince Yadav (2 for 20) delivered a phenomenal three-over spell to dismiss Pathum Nissanka and heavily restrict the scoring. In a much-talked-about move, LSG captain Rishabh Pant opened the batting but suffered horrible luck, getting run out at the non-striker's end for 7 (9) on a straight drive. Firing up the Lucknow crowd, Shami dismissed Delhi's newly acquired opener KL Rahul for a golden duck on the very first ball of the innings. Chasing a modest 142 on a tricky Ekana Stadium surface, the Capitals found themselves reeling in the Powerplay before an unbroken century partnership between Sameer Rizvi and Tristan Stubbs guided them home with 17 deliveries to spare. His decision paid immediate dividends as the Capitals consistently chipped away at the Super Giants' batting lineup, eventually bowling them out for 141 in 18.4 overs. Just as the Super Giants tightened their grip on the contest, Impact Player Sameer Rizvi and Tristan Stubbs launched a methodical, match-winning counter-attack. Meanwhile, the Lucknow Super Giants will look to address their batting frailties and build upon their fierce Powerplay bowling ahead of their next fixture. The match also served a dramatic narrative twist, pitting Rishabh Pant and KL Rahul against their former franchises in a highly anticipated encounter. Mitchell Marsh attempted to counter-attack, muscling his way to a team-high 35 off 28 deliveries, including three towering sixes. With this composed victory, the Delhi Capitals bounce back strongly to establish early momentum in their TATA IPL 2026 campaign. The Super Giants' pace attack capitalised on this monumental breakthrough, tearing through the Capitals' top order. Lungi Ngidi produced a pace-off masterclass to finish with outstanding figures of 3 for 27 in 3.4 overs, famously bamboozling Nicholas Pooran with a gorgeous slower delivery. Dhoni was initially ruled out for the first two weeks of the season following a calf strain whereas Brevis missed the Rajasthan Royals clash with a side strain with no clear information on his return. While uncertainty still remains over their participation in the Punjab Kings fixture, their return to the nets bodes well for a batting unit that clearly lacked experience in its first game against Rajasthan Royals. CSK have a short turnaround following the PBKS fixture as they take on defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday (April 5). When asked about Dhoni's rehabilitation progress, Gaikwad chuckled claiming an update would be provided on the 13th day given the former skipper was ruled out for a period of two weeks. However, minutes after CSK kickstarted their practice session, Brevis was spotted in a separate nets facility where he batted with no visible discomfort. Ahead of their first match at Chepauk in IPL 2026, captain Ruturaj Gaikwad remained noncommittal about both players. But just before CSK concluded their practice session, Dhoni walked out, took throwdowns in the nets and smashed several deliveries into the stands. In Brevis' absence, CSK handed Australia's Matthew Short an opportunity at No.4 whereas Sarfaraz Khan broke into the middle order as an impact sub. Brevis was, however, doing short-run training in the practise session ahead of the Royals game with the team physios. Match 4 - PBKS vs GT IPL MEDIA ADVISORY April 01, 2026 Match 4, PBKS vs GT - Code of Conduct Punjab Kings (PBKS) Captain Shreyas Iyer has been fined after his team maintained a slow over-rate during Match No. 4 of the TATA Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 against Gujarat Titans (GT) at the New International Cricket Stadium, New Chandigarh. As this was his team’s first offence of the season under Article 2.22 of the IPL’s Code of Conduct, which pertains to minimum over-rate offences, Iyer was fined INR 12 lakhs. How good was he timing the ball 👌👌 Priyansh Arya looked in terrific touch 🎶 He goes back for an entertaining 39(11) 👏👏 Updates ▶️ https://t.co/riWrE2EZiF #TATAIPL | #KhelBindaas | #CSKvPBKS pic.twitter.com/BW7Qdd36fc — IndianPremierLeague (@IPL) April 3, 2026 Arya’s explosive knock came to an end at 39 off just 11 balls (three fours and four sixes), dismissed by Matt Henry with PBKS cruising at 61/1 in 4.2 overs. Updates ▶️ https://t.co/riWrE2EZiF #TATAIPL | #KhelBindaas | #CSKvPBKS pic.twitter.com/UyJtggrhxT — IndianPremierLeague (@IPL) April 3, 2026 The CSK skipper eventually fell for 28 off 22 balls, holing out in the deep off Yuzvendra Chahal as he looked to accelerate. The win marked Punjab Kings’ second consecutive victory to begin their campaign, while Chennai Super Kings are yet to open their account this season. Their late surge ensured CSK plundered 99 runs in the remaining 8.5 overs after Gaikwad’s dismissal, finishing with a formidable 209/5, which is their second-highest total against Punjab in IPL history. Vijaykumar Vyshak picked up two key wickets, dismissing both Mhatre and Sarfaraz, but the rest of the bowling attack struggled to contain the flow of runs. Sanju Samson fell cheaply for 7, but skipper Ruturaj Gaikwad and the 18-year-old Ayush Mhatre steadied the innings with an impressive partnership. Cooper Connolly added further impetus with a brisk 38 off 22 balls, but it was skipper Shreyas Iyer who controlled the chase in the middle overs. After winning the toss, PBKS opted to bowl but were made to toil early as CSK recovered well from an early setback. For PBKS, Yuzvendra Chahal stood out with a tidy spell of 1/21 in his three overs. Shivam Dube provided the finishing touch with a composed unbeaten 45 off 27 balls, while Sarfaraz Khan injected momentum with a blistering 32 off just 12 deliveries, striking six fours and a six. However, with Marcus Stoinis and Shashank Singh at the crease, Punjab held their composure to close out the chase comfortably, finishing with five wickets in hand and 17 balls to spare. Prabhsimran Singh, who had initially played second fiddle, then anchored the innings with a steady 43 off 34 balls before being run out in the ninth over. Matt Henry (2/54) and Anshul Kamboj (2/43) were the only CSK bowlers to get into the wickets column on a tough night for the hosts. Priyansh Arya was named Player of the Match for his electrifying start at the top, which laid the foundation for PBKS’ chase. Despite losing both set batters in quick succession, CSK maintained their attacking approach in the death overs. When Iyer and Nehal Wadhera fell off successive deliveries, PBKS needed 24 runs from 17 balls. 🏎️ The youngster putting wheels to @ChennaiIPL 's innings 🔥 #CSK 57/1 after the Powerplay. The duo added 96 runs off just 55 balls, shifting momentum firmly in CSK’s favour. His well-paced 50 off 29 balls ensured PBKS stayed ahead of the required rate. At 110/1 in 11.1 overs, CSK were well placed for a big finish. In response, PBKS came out with aggressive intent.

Through this initiative, Delhi Capitals aims to contribute meaningfully to the city’s cricketing ecosystem and inspire the champions of tomorrow. More than a tournament, the DC School Cup is a long-term commitment to embedding DC deeper into Delhi’s cultural fabric, engaging schools, empowering young athletes and building a credible pathway from grassroots to higher-level cricket. With BCCI-qualified officials, curated venues, white-ball cricket, team kits and a full match-day setup, the DC School Cup elevates school-level cricket to a standard young athletes can aspire to.

As this was his team’s second offence of the season under Article 2.22 of the IPL’s Code of Conduct, which pertains to minimum over-rate offences, Iyer was fined INR 24 lakhs. The rest of the members of the Playing XI, including the Impact Player, will be fined either INR six lakhs or 25 per cent of their respective match fees, whichever is lesser. Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) LSG await Hasaranga clarity, Pathirana's return in sight for KKR, IPL 2026 | Cricbuzz.com LSG await Hasaranga clarity, Pathirana's return in sight for KKR There is still no clarity on Wanindu Hasaranga's participation in IPL 2026 for Lucknow Super Giants, but there are encouraging signs that Matheesha Pathirana could turn out for the Kolkata Knight Riders during the ongoing season. A similar response came from a Lucknow Super Giants source, who said the franchise has no clear information and it would not be a surprise if they initiate a move to seek a replacement. Meanwhile, it is learnt that Pathirana has been making progress, and his franchise, Kolkata Knight Riders, who bought him for INR 18 crore, remain confident that he will eventually be available this season. Following a complaint from Sunrisers Hyderabad in 2024, the IPL introduced provisions for sanctions on overseas players who pull out after the auction. Hasaranga, an INR 2 crore base-price buy for Lucknow Super Giants, has not only failed to report to the franchise but has also not given any indication of when he is likely to arrive. It is learnt that Hasaranga (28) is yet to undergo the fitness test that Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) intends to conduct before issuing its players NOCs for the IPL. All these overseas spinners are in the RAPP list, from which a team can seek a replacement. "We don't have any concrete update on Wanindu Hasaranga," said a source close to him.

He is almost certain to miss the Delhi Capitals game, and the odds are stacked against him even to make the KKR match, even as CSK await their former captain to add his experience to a campaign that has nosedived in the first three games. Over the next seven days, the Super Kings are scheduled to host a couple teams at the Chepauk - Delhi Capitals on April 11 and Kolkata Knight Riders on April 14. © AFP MS Dhoni's fitness situation continues to be a cause for concern for Chennai Super Kings. The CSK talisman is nowhere near match fitness as of Tuesday (April 7), and there is no immediate clarity on when he will be in a position to take the field for the next game, against Delhi Capitals on April 11. Cricbuzz understands that Dhoni is currently in Chennai with his family, undergoing rehabilitation under the supervision of dedicated CSK support staff, including a physio and a masseur. CSK officials confirm that Dhoni has been undergoing rehab for a calf muscle strain, and at this stage, he may need at least one more week to be fit enough to play. "So, I think lots of us out there young cricketers and old cricketers are never too old to learn a new skill," Crowe continued, "and the fact that in four days they learned that new skill, delivered that ball and got Abhishek out, I think it's testament to not only Bharat, his coaching, but also Mohammed Shami's mindset that, as a senior player, he's still very willing to develop new skills, trying to push his game." Against SRH, Shami completed his quota in the ninth over itself, also using the off-pace variation to dismiss Travis Head and finishing with figures of 4-0-9-2, the most economical spell bowled by anyone since IPL 2021. © BCCI Mohammed Shami knocked over Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head with slower balls. Speaking on Wednesday (April 8), a day ahead of their fixture against KKR, Carl Crowe said that Shami worked on the new variation with fast bowling coach Bharat Arun just ahead of their fixture in Hyderabad. "Would you believe Bharat and Shami worked on a new ball four days before the game, and Shami bowled that ball to dismiss Abhishek," Crowe said. Shami, Prince Yadav and Avesh Khan were all amongst the wickets, but there was no space for Mayank Yadav in the XI. Cricbuzz.com works to provide you with blazing fast, reliable and accurate cricket updates of all live cricket news, international cricket matches as well as the cricket leagues from around the world (Indian Premier League, Big Bash League, Champions League T20 .. He praised Shami for continuing to learn new tricks even late into his career and continuing to be effective, in conjunction with Arun, who has worked with him as India's fast bowling coach as well. "Yeah, the beauty of the squad we have is we've got a fantastic range of fast bowlers and it's a luxury to have so many great bowlers at the moment," Crowe said. When asked if Shami will get a role at the death, or continue to utilised primarily in the Powerplay, Crowe refused to give a definite answer. "The one thing I do think is when Mohamed Shami's on the run, he's very difficult to play. So, if the ball is swinging, you're going to use it to his strengths". The previous ball, Shami had taken out the first slip.

title: George Linde named as replacement for injured Wanindu Hasaranga for TATA IPL 2026
raw_text: George Linde to replace Wanindu Hasaranga in IPL 2026 for Lucknow Super Giants TATA IPL 2026: LSG vs GT preview - Time to carry the momentum and make it three on the spin George Linde named as replacement for injured Wanindu Hasaranga for TATA IPL 2026 IPL 2026 | KKR vs LSG | Match Gallery TATA IPL 2026: KKR vs LSG report - Mukul’s heroics pull off last-ball heist in Kolkata Follow us on Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube LinkedIn IOS Android Login View Profile Logout Home Lucknow Super Giants Home Matches Standings Latest News & Features Photos Team IPL Auction 2026 Shop Follow us on Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube LinkedIn IOS Android Home News George Linde to replace Wanindu Hasaranga in IPL 2026 for Lucknow Super Giants George Linde named as replacement for injured Wanindu Hasaranga for TATA IPL 2026 10 Apr, 2026 News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close South Africa’s left-arm spinner George Linde is ready to make his way into the Lucknow Super Giants squad at the expense of Wanindu Hasaranga, who is now ruled out due to an injury. South Africa’s left-arm spinner George Linde is ready to make his way into the Lucknow Super Giants squad at the expense of Wanindu Hasaranga, who is now ruled out due to an injury.Bought at his base price of INR 2.0 crore in the mini-auction held in December last year, the Sri Lankan all-rounder was all set to play his fifth TATA IPL campaign before an unfortunate setback had its say.Talking about George Linde’s T20 exploits, the experienced 34-year-old has featured in more than 200 matches in the shortest format of the game, racking up 2,700+ runs in addition to 200+ wickets at an economy of 7.42.He now joins our potent bowling unit alongside the likes of Digvesh Rathi and Manimaran Siddharth, among other spinners, bolstering the spin bowling options.George Linde, welcome to the Lucknow Super Giants! George Linde to replace Wanindu Hasaranga in IPL 2026 for Lucknow Super Giants Left-arm spinner George Linde is set to replace injured Wanindu Hasaranga for Lucknow Super Giants in the TATA IPL 2026. On behalf of the Super Giants Family, we wish Wanindu Hasaranga a speedy recovery! However, when asked if he has received enough plaudits during the good times, Fleming chose to credit the players and MS Dhoni instead. "It's actually purely the players," Fleming said ahead of CSK's fourth clash of the IPL 2026 season in the quest for their maiden win. Probable XII: Rohit Sharma, Ryan Rickelton (wk), Tilak Varma, Suryakumar Yadav, Hardik Pandya (c), Naman Dhir, Sherfane Rutherford, Mitchell Santner, Shardul Thakur, Deepak Chahar, Trent Boult, Jasprit Bumrah Royal Challengers Bengaluru Injuries/Unavailabilities: Having travelled from Guwahati to Mumbai on Saturday, RCB chose rest over practice. A clash between Mumbai Indians and Royal Challengers Bengaluru is always a mouth-watering one, if not quite the El Clasico - an honour generally reserved for the season's face-offs between Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings. I look at it as Mumbai Indians versus Royal Challengers Bengaluru, and let the individuals enjoy this phase of their careers," the MI batting coach said, playing down the emphasis on individuals in a team game. Team Watch Mumbai Indians Injuries/Unavailabilities: Will Jacks is yet to report but Kieron Pollard confirms Mitchell Santner, who missed the previous match, is available for the game. But the new Ro-Ko factor adds a new dimension to the contest, with both sides coming off a mauling at the hands of a Rajasthan Royals unit powered by a certain Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in their respective last matches. Tactics & Match-ups: Devdutt Padikkal and Tim David have been the dangermen for RCB, and it would not be a surprise if MI manage Jasprit Bumrah's overs carefully with the two in mind. Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli remain box office, as compelling as rivals as they are as teammates, and this promises to be a blockbuster contest on Sunday night in Mumbai. Tactics & Match-ups: Jacob Bethell in place of Phil Salt is a probable option for RCB although it will be unfair on Salt who did well against CSK in the second match. They need to capitalise on their familiar home conditions - something important in the IPL and something RCB have managed to do, so far. Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) Zalmi continue unbeaten run, Kingsmen secure first PSL win | Cricbuzz.com Zalmi continue unbeaten run, Kingsmen secure first PSL win Peshawar Zalmi vs Lahore Qalandars After trouncing the Karachi Kings in their previous fixture, Peshawar Zalmi extended their unbeaten run by clinching another commanding 76-run win over Lahore Qalandars on Saturday. Cricbuzz.com works to provide you with blazing fast, reliable and accurate cricket updates of all live cricket news, international cricket matches as well as the cricket leagues from around the world (Indian Premier League, Big Bash League, Champions League T20 .. Brief Scores: Peshawar Zalmi 173/7 in 20 overs (Kusal Mendis 74, Babar Azam 43; Shaheen Afridi 3-22) beat Lahore Qalandars 97 all-out in 17 overs (Abdullah Shafique 21, Fakhar Zaman 21; Michael Bracewell 3-18, Sufiyan Muqeem 3-21) by 76 runs. Brief Scores: Karachi Kings 188/8 in 20 overs (Saad Baig 53, Moeen Ali 44; Hunain Shah 3-19, Mohammad Ali 2-35) lost to Hyderabad Kingsmen 189/6 in 19.1 overs (Hassan Khan 33*, Irfan Khan 29*; Abbas Afridi 2-30) by four wickets. Two excellent overs at the death from Hunain Shah, who was the pick of the bowlers with figures of 3-19, helped the Kings regain control as only 22 runs were conceded off the last three overs. Karachi Kings vs Hyderabad Kingsmen An epic 24-ball 50-run stand between Irfan Khan and Hassan Khan secured the Hyderabad Kingsmen's first win in the PSL. Chasing 189, the Kingsmen started well with Marnus Labuschagne (26), who opened the innings, Maaz Sadaqat (37) and Usman Khan (27) contributing. Earlier, Saad Baig's fifty laid the platform for the Kings before Moeen Ali's 16-ball 44-run blitz threatened to take them well past 200. They stumbled at the death with wickets tumbling in a heap but Michael Bracewell's 10-ball 21 ensured they finished with 173/7. Asked to bat first again, Zalmi's batting effort was led by Kusal Mendis (74) and Babar Azam (43) once more. Michael Bracewell and Sufiyan Muqeem picked up three wickets each while Nahid Rana scalped two.

Here are the Impact substitutes for both the teams: Royal Challengers Bengaluru Impact Subs: Rasikh Salam Dar, Venkatesh Iyer, Jacob Bethell, Swapnil Singh, Mangesh Yadav Mumbai Indians Impact Subs: Sherfane Rutherford, Corbin Bosch, Ashwani Kumar, Raghu Sharma, Raj Bawa LSG vs GT, MI vs RCB | IPL 2026 (Hindi) Rajat Patidar: Obviously would have bowled first. Probable XII: Virat Kohli, Phil Salt/Jacob Bethell, Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar (c), Jitesh Sharma (wk), Tim David, Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Abhinandan Singh, Josh Hazlewood, Suyash Sharma Did you know Jasprit Bumrah has not picked a wicket in his last four IPL games, a sequence stretching back to the second Qualifier last season. A clash between Mumbai Indians and Royal Challengers Bengaluru is always a mouth-watering one, if not quite the El Clasico - an honour generally reserved for the season's face-offs between Mumbai Indians and Chennai Super Kings. If Mumbai Indians boast Rohit Sharma, Royal Challengers Bengaluru fire back with Virat Kohli. I look at it as Mumbai Indians versus Royal Challengers Bengaluru, and let the individuals enjoy this phase of their careers," the MI batting coach said, playing down the emphasis on individuals in a team game. Team Watch Mumbai Indians Injuries/Unavailabilities: Will Jacks is yet to report but Kieron Pollard confirms Mitchell Santner, who missed the previous match, is available for the game. Hardik meets Krunal, Suryakumar Yadav takes on Rajat Patidar, while Jasprit Bumrah goes head-to-head with Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Preview: Mumbai vs Bengaluru Match Forecast: IPL - MI vs RCB, 20th Match Preview: Mumbai v Bengaluru (हिन्दी) Preview by Vijay Tagore It's Ro-Ko time in the IPL and if the broadcasters have not quite made a big splash of it yet, it could be because the two will be on opposite sides of the duel at the Wankhede. But the new Ro-Ko factor adds a new dimension to the contest, with both sides coming off a mauling at the hands of a Rajasthan Royals unit powered by a certain Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in their respective last matches. Tactics & Match-ups: Devdutt Padikkal and Tim David have been the dangermen for RCB, and it would not be a surprise if MI manage Jasprit Bumrah's overs carefully with the two in mind. Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli remain box office, as compelling as rivals as they are as teammates, and this promises to be a blockbuster contest on Sunday night in Mumbai. Tactics & Match-ups: Jacob Bethell in place of Phil Salt is a probable option for RCB although it will be unfair on Salt who did well against CSK in the second match. They need to capitalise on their familiar home conditions - something important in the IPL and something RCB have managed to do, so far. Mumbai Indians began their campaign with a win over Kolkata Knight Riders, but have since hit a rough patch with two straight defeats.

title: Skiddy’s four-fer and Gill-Buttler show seal back-to-back wins
raw_text: TATA IPL 2026: Dominant Titans beat Super Giants to register second consecutive win Gujarat Titans Our Titans target back-to-back wins in Lucknow showdown Titans fight back to register first win of the season in Delhi Gujarat Titans eye their first win of the season against Delhi Capitals Follow On Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube Login -Pts View Profile Logout Home Tickets Hand Cricket GT Rewards Fixtures News More Standings Stats Photos Videos Squad Search Search Shop Whatsapp Home News TATA IPL 2026: Dominant Titans beat Super Giants to register second consecutive win Skiddy’s four-fer and Gill-Buttler show seal back-to-back wins Prasidh Krishna, Shubman Gill and Jos Buttler were our standout performers in our fifth game of the TATA IPL 2026 against Lucknow Super Giants on Sunday. Related tags: Shubman Gill Jos Buttler Prasidh Krishna Washington Sundar Ashok Sharma 2026 News Our Titans target back-to-back wins in Lucknow showdown News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Titans fight back to register first win of the season in Delhi News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Gujarat Titans eye their first win of the season against Delhi Capitals News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Sai Su delivers batting masterclass but Titans fall short in last-ball thriller News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Principal Partner Associate Partners --> --> Official Partners Merchandise Partners Kit Partner Medical Partner --> Radio Partners Official Ticketing Partner © Copyright Gujarat Titans. raw_text: IPL 2026 | LSG v GT | Match Gallery TATA IPL 2026: LSG vs GT report - Not the result we wanted at Ekana TATA IPL 2026: LSG vs GT preview - Time to carry the momentum and make it three on the spin Follow us on Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube LinkedIn IOS Android Login View Profile Logout Home Lucknow Super Giants Home Matches Standings Latest News & Features Photos Team IPL Auction 2026 Shop Follow us on Facebook Instagram Twitter YouTube LinkedIn IOS Android Home News George Linde to replace Wanindu Hasaranga in IPL 2026 for Lucknow Super Giants George Linde named as replacement for injured Wanindu Hasaranga for TATA IPL 2026 10 Apr, 2026 News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close South Africa’s left-arm spinner George Linde is ready to make his way into the Lucknow Super Giants squad at the expense of Wanindu Hasaranga, who is now ruled out due to an injury. South Africa’s left-arm spinner George Linde is ready to make his way into the Lucknow Super Giants squad at the expense of Wanindu Hasaranga, who is now ruled out due to an injury.Bought at his base price of INR 2.0 crore in the mini-auction held in December last year, the Sri Lankan all-rounder was all set to play his fifth TATA IPL campaign before an unfortunate setback had its say.Talking about George Linde’s T20 exploits, the experienced 34-year-old has featured in more than 200 matches in the shortest format of the game, racking up 2,700+ runs in addition to 200+ wickets at an economy of 7.42.He now joins our potent bowling unit alongside the likes of Digvesh Rathi and Manimaran Siddharth, among other spinners, bolstering the spin bowling options.George Linde, welcome to the Lucknow Super Giants! title: MI vs RCB, Match 20, IPL 2026, Review
raw_text: NEXT I was bowling to take wickets – Suyash Sharma on his aggressive approach against MI 13 Apr Pure team effort – Rajat Patidar on RCB’s clinical win against MI 13 Apr RCB TV TRENDING MI vs RCB: Big Basket Player of the Match, Rasikh Salam | IPL 2026 13 Apr MI vs RCB: Nothing Player of the Match, Phil Salt | IPL 2026 13 Apr MI vs RCB: Fans Reaction after the Win at Wankhede | IPL 2026 13 Apr MI vs RCB: Post Match Celebrations | IPL 2026 | Game Day 13 Apr MI vs RCB: Sunday Blockbuster, Match Preview | IPL 2026 | Game Day 12 Apr Footer menu Home About Us News Fixtures PRIVACY AND COOKIE NOTICE Rcb Tv Team Terms & Conditions Shop T&C Photos Shop Contact Us Connect with RCB Players on the RCB App. Download Now Submitted by Amith Chakrapani on Mon, 04/13/2026 - 14:27 RCB return to winning ways at the expense of Mumbai Indians in thrilling run fest 13 Apr, 2026 Editor RCB return to winning ways at the expense of Mumbai Indians in thrilling run fest 13 Apr, 2026 By Editor The Royal Challengers Bengaluru returned to winning ways, blowing out Mumbai Indians in a high-scoring encounter in Wankhede. A Phil Salt special combined with Rajat Patidar’s smash and grab knock saw RCB sailing hammer and tongs across the finish line in front of a packed house in Mumbai. The fall of Salt saw a change in RCB’s batting order as their captain, Rajat Patidar got a promotion. The RCB captain brought up his second consecutive half-century in just 17 balls, carrying the same rich vein of form since the start of the fray. For the fourth time in the last four games, RCB managed to cross the 200-run-barrier, setting a target of 241 for the hosts. However, it hardly could be the spanner in RCB’s plans as Rajat Patidar maintained the tempo at the other end. Phil Salt and Virat Kohli hunkered down to business straight away and would only deal in boundaries. --> MI vs RCB, Match 20, IPL 2026, Review Skip to main content Download Our App       #Playbold 0 Download Our App Enter your keywords Test #Playbold Main navigation Home News Rcb Tv Team Fixtures RCB BAR & CAFE TICKETS Shop More DIGITAL DAY Sports Report INVESTORS Resignation Letter- Shilpa Vaid Resignation Letter- VK Viswanathan Annual Return FY 2024-2025 CSR Project approved by Board for 2025-26 CSR POLICY Resignation Letter – Prathmesh Annual Return FY2023-2024 Annual Return FY2022-2023 Annual Return FY2021-2022 Go Green Photos About Us Privacy Policy Terms & Conditions Shop T&C Contact Us Connect with RCB Players on the RCB App. The departure of Patidar slowed things down for the Royal Challengers as in the final four overs they could only garner 46 runs, which by the standards of Patidar and company felt like a fall from the fifth gear in which they operated. Virat Kohli hung in at the other end and eventually brought up his half-century from 37 balls, gliding one towards deep-square leg against Hardik Pandya. Much to the shock of the Mumbai fans, Sharma had to retire hurt after restricted movements in the fifth over and that allowed RCB to force their way in. Much to his woes, he fell in that very over against Mitchell Santner for a 20-ball-53 as RCB were cruising at 194/3 from 16 overs. Put to bat first, RCB had no dilemma about starting in the fifth gear as the season saw its first Salt Assault. A googly floated up saw Rickelton slashing at it and his skier nestled in the hands of Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Tim David stayed unbeaten for 34 off just 16 balls while Jitesh Sharma took some time to get a move on. Jacob Duffy came in place of Josh Hazlewood while Suyash Sharma came in place of Abhinandan Singh. The stand added 47 runs for the third wicket before Krunal Pandya struck to dismiss SKY (33). RCB totted up 71 runs from their powerplay as Salt feasted upon the Mumbai bowling attack. The following free-hit as well saw Sherfane Rutherford being caught by Rajat Patidar. RCB had two changes to the side from the side that faced Rajasthan Royals. RCB secured a win by 18 runs to make it three out of four wins. Phil Salt won the Player of the Match award for his 36-ball-78. Download Now Copyright Royal Challengers Sports Pvt.

Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) Rohit Sharma set for scans on suspected hamstring injury IPL 2026 Mumbai Indians | Cricbuzz.com Rohit Sharma set for scans on suspected hamstring injury Rohit Sharma will undergo scans to understand the severity of his hamstring injury, Cricbuzz understands. The star batter's availability for Mumbai Indians' next match, against Punjab Kings on Thursday (April 16) will depend on the results of those scans. Rohit, 38, retired hurt during Mumbai Indians' match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday night, walking off in the sixth over. © AFP Rohit Sharma will undergo scans to understand the severity of his hamstring injury, Cricbuzz understands. Virat is fine, says Krunal Virat Kohli was also seen resting in the dressing room after RCB's batting innings. It was revealed that Kohli was sidelined from taking the field during MI chase because of an ankle injury. © AFP Rohit Sharma walked off retired hurt for a 13-ball 19 against RCB. Cricbuzz.com works to provide you with blazing fast, reliable and accurate cricket updates of all live cricket news, international cricket matches as well as the cricket leagues from around the world (Indian Premier League, Big Bash League, Champions League T20 .. Mumbai Indians, who have lost three of their four games, will need the experience of their talisman, who recently completed 15 years with the franchise. Rohit has scored 137 runs in four matches, including a half-century, and looked in good touch on Sunday night before the injury setback. There was a lengthy stoppage for treatment before he returned to face the second ball, only to block it and walk off retired hurt. "I mean, I have not spoken to the physio yet, but I think he'll be fine," Pandya said after RCB won the game by 18 runs. Before retiring hurt, Rohit was attended to by the Mumbai Indians physio before the first delivery of that over. The exact status of Rohit will be clear only after the scan results reveal the extent of the injury. He walked off after facing the second ball from Rasikh Salam in RCB's final Powerplay over. But teammate Krunal Pandya allayed fears of the injury being too serious.

It was an amazing comeback by Peshawar Zalmi, who stayed calm and pulled the game back just when it looked like they might lose. By the 15th over, they were in a great position thanks to good batting from Shan Masood, Josh Philippe, and Steve Smith. The Multan batters fell apart under pressure, managed to score only 27 more runs, and lost 4 important wickets. 18.6 Nahid Rana to Mohammad Wasim Jr, wide , back of a length delivery down the leg. Zalmi have been penalized for slow over-rate.

7.4 Krunal Pandya to Mitchell Marsh, wide , there you go, the bouncer banged in short on the stump, this one really got up and wide signalled 7.3 Krunal Pandya to Ayush Badoni, 1 run, quicker and on the pads, Badoni flicks it off his legs to deep square leg 7.2 Krunal Pandya to Mitchell Marsh, 1 run, slower through the air and on middle, Marsh pushes it to long-off 7.1 Krunal Pandya to Ayush Badoni, 1 run, sliding on the pads, Badoni works it to deep square leg Krunal Pandya, Left arm orthodox, comes into the attack Over 7 40-2 W 4 L1 0 0 0 ( 5 runs) Mitchell Marsh 22 (22) Ayush Badoni 4 (2) Josh Hazlewood 3-0-10-1 Over Summary View all overs 6.6 Josh Hazlewood to Mitchell Marsh, no run, he's not able to connect anything today, another cross-seam delivery, Marsh was bit early into the shot and the ball just went over the off stump, fantastic stuff from Hazlewood 6.5 Josh Hazlewood to Mitchell Marsh, no run, ouch that must have hurt, back of a length and this one hurried onto him as he was looking for the pull shot, missed it and is hit flush on the box 6.4 Josh Hazlewood to Mitchell Marsh, no run, tailing into the pads, Marsh works it to short midwicket 6.3 Josh Hazlewood to Ayush Badoni, leg byes, 1 run, straying on the pads, he's missed out on this one as the ball trickles off the pads towards square leg 6.2 4 Josh Hazlewood to Ayush Badoni, FOUR , he's off the mark, back of a length and in the channel outside off, first bad delivery from Hazlewood, Badoni stays back and guides it in the gap to third man, the deep fielder had no chance Ayush Badoni, right handed bat, comes to the crease 6.1 W Josh Hazlewood to Nicholas Pooran, out Bowled!! Over 6 35-1 0 1 0 0 0 1 ( 2 runs) Nicholas Pooran 1 (6) Mitchell Marsh 22 (19) Rasikh Salam Dar 2-0-12-1 Over Summary View all overs 5.6 Rasikh Salam Dar to Nicholas Pooran, 1 run, full and outside off, Pooran digs it to cover, lowest power play score for LSG in this season. raw_text: Talking in the post-match presentation, Kohli opened up on his fitness and his recovery from the fever he experienced. --> “I was happy with my intensity” – Virat Kohli on his phenomenal knock against LSG in IPL 2026 Skip to main content Download Our App       #Playbold 0 Download Our App Enter your keywords Test #Playbold Main navigation Home News Rcb Tv Team Fixtures RCB BAR & CAFE TICKETS Shop More DIGITAL DAY Sports Report INVESTORS Resignation Letter- Shilpa Vaid Resignation Letter- VK Viswanathan Annual Return FY 2024-2025 CSR Project approved by Board for 2025-26 CSR POLICY Resignation Letter – Prathmesh Annual Return FY2023-2024 Annual Return FY2022-2023 Annual Return FY2021-2022 Go Green Photos About Us Privacy Policy Terms & Conditions Shop T&C Contact Us Connect with RCB Players on the RCB App. Download Now Submitted by Amith Chakrapani on Thu, 04/16/2026 - 14:19 I was happy with my intensity – Virat Kohli on his defining knock against LSG 16 Apr, 2026 Editor I was happy with my intensity – Virat Kohli on his defining knock against LSG 16 Apr, 2026 By Editor Batting with an injury requires significant heart and endurance. Sharing an update on his fitness, Kohli said, “Much better than the last game. Luckily for RCB, Virat Kohli has that in abundance. I was able to put the team in front early on.” RCB TV TRENDING Travel Diaries: Mumbai to Bengaluru | IPL 2026 | RCB Bold Diaries 14 Apr MI vs RCB: Lucky Fan Receives Signed Jersey from Krunal Pandya | IPL 2026 14 Apr Big Basket Player of the Match vs Chennai, Suyash Sharma | IPL 2026 | RCB Game Day 05 Apr MI vs RCB: Parody Press Conference with Mr Nags | IPL 2026 | RCB Insider 13 Apr MI vs RCB: Big Basket Player of the Match, Rasikh Salam | IPL 2026 13 Apr Footer menu Home About Us News Fixtures PRIVACY AND COOKIE NOTICE Rcb Tv Team Terms & Conditions Shop T&C Photos Shop Contact Us Connect with RCB Players on the RCB App. After sustaining an injury in the game against the Mumbai Indians, Kohli stepped in as an impact player against the Lucknow Super Giants and showed why he is still one of the greatest players to have ever played the game. When Phil Salt fell early, Kohli stepped up and dropped his own signature masterclass, hammering 49 off just 34 balls, a knock comprising six fours and a six. He also explained the conditions in play at Chinnaswamy as RCB ran away with the game after the bowlers put in a solid shift, restricting LSG to 146. Chasing 147 against LSG, RCB needed a couple of good knocks and the game was done. Download Now Copyright Royal Challengers Sports Pvt.

Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) Ankle injury rules SRH David Payne out of IPL 2026 IPL 2026 Sunrisers Hyderabad injury list | Cricbuzz.com Ankle injury rules David Payne out of IPL 2026 Sunrisers Hyderabad's left-arm seamer David Payne has been ruled out of the remainder of IPL 2026 with an ankle injury, adding to the franchise's growing list of absentees. SRH head into their next fixture against Chennai Super Kings in Hyderabad on the back of a win over the Royals, powered by impressive four-fers from IPL debutants Praful Hinge and Sakib Hussain. The 35-year-old Payne featured in only two games this season, returning figures of 2 for 35 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the season opener and 0 for 35 against Kolkata Knight Riders. © BCCI/IPL Sunrisers Hyderabad's left-arm seamer David Payne has been ruled out of the remainder of IPL 2026 with an ankle injury, adding to the franchise's growing list of absentees. Captain Pat Cummins has been cleared to rejoin the squad after undergoing scans on his back in Sydney. In Cummins' absence, Ishan Kishan has led the side, with SRH currently placed fourth on the points table with two wins from five games. Cummins is yet to play this season but is targeting a potential return against Rajasthan Royals in Jaipur on April 25. Payne had joined the squad as a replacement for Jack Edwards, who was earlier sidelined with a foot injury. England allrounder Brydon Carse was also ruled out recently after suffering a hand injury during a net session at the start of the season. He did not feature in a game, prompting SRH to rope in Sri Lanka quick Dilshan Madushanka as his replacement. © BCCI/IPL Payne had joined SRH as a replacement for Jack Edwards. However, there is some positive news for SRH on the injury front. SRH haven't yet named a replacement for Payne. Tamim also announced health benefits for captains which includes medical support equivalent to that of current national players. Tamim Iqbal, former national captain and current BCB ad-hoc body head, took the initiative of honouring the captains in a city hotel and described it as a small but meaningful step towards recognizing their effort and contribution towards the game of cricket in Bangladesh. Tamim added that captains' card will serve as a lifetime access pass to all cricket events in Bangladesh, including international matches, ICC and ACC tournaments, the Bangladesh Premier League, and domestic competitions. Tamim added that he had personally reached out to several former captains who were unable to attend the event, including Mashrafe Bin Mortaza, Shakib Al Hasan, Aminul Islam Bulbul and Naimur Rahman Durjoy. BCB offered a range of benefits to 33 captains -- 27 from the men's team and six from the women's team -- who have led Bangladesh across formats since 1977. © Cricbuzz BCB offered a range of benefits to 33 captains -- 27 from the men's team and six from the women's team. Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) Connor Esterhuizen replaces injured Tom Banton at GT, IPL 2026 | Cricbuzz.com Connor Esterhuizen replaces injured Tom Banton at GT South African wicket-keeper batter Connor Esterhuizen has been signed by Gujarat Titans for the remainder of IPL 2026. The Titans, who have won and lost two games apiece, face bottom-placed Kolkata Knight Riders in Ahmedabad on Friday (April 17). He will join GT at his base price of INR 75 lakh and will serve as a backup wicket-keeping option for Jos Buttler. Esterhuizen replaces England's Tom Banton, who has been ruled out of the tournament with a finger injury. Esterhuizen has also represented MI Cape Town and more lately, Pretoria Capitals in the SA20. He travelled with the squad to Kolkata before returning to Australia, where he underwent a fitness test and will now rejoin the SRH squad. While Vettori was pleased with the bowling show put on by the two in the previous game, the head coach hinted that there could be some changes to the combination in the game against CSK, based on the conditions. Coetzee, who replaces Payne at SRH, has played 14 IPL games in the past - representing Gujarat Titans (GT) and Mumbai Indians (MI), and has 15 IPL wickets. Payne - who played 2 games for SRH in the ongoing IPL season and picked 2 wickets from the same - is ruled out of the remainder of the season due to an ankle injury. title: Captain Gill, KG power Titans to hat-trick of wins
raw_text: Brief Scores : Kolkata Knight Riders 180/10 in 20 overs (Cameron Green 79, Rovman Powell 27; Kagiso Rabada 3/29) lost to Gujarat Titans 181/5 in 19.4 overs (Shubman Gill 86, Jos Buttler 25; Varun Chakaravarthy 2/34) by 5 wickets Related tags: Sai Sudharsan Shubman Gill Kagiso Rabada Jos Buttler Prasidh Krishna Mohammed Siraj News Titans chase a hat-trick of wins as we welcome KKR to Gujaratno Gadh News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Titans’ day out at FRIZBee: Sai Su, Washi, GP and KG win hearts in fun-filled event News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Update Your Calendars: Our TATA IPL 2026 fixtures against CSK have been swapped News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Skiddy’s four-fer and Gill-Buttler show seal back-to-back wins News Share Share Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on WhatsApp Copy Link Close Principal Partner Associate Partners --> --> Official Partners Merchandise Partners Kit Partner Medical Partner --> Radio Partners Official Ticketing Partner © Copyright Gujarat Titans. Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) SRH pick Gerald Coetzee as injury replacement for David Payne ipl 2026 | Cricbuzz.com SRH pick Gerald Coetzee as injury replacement for David Payne Sunrisers Hyderabad have signed South Africa pacer Gerald Coetzee as an injury replacement for David Payne. He joined their squad on Friday, April 17 and will be available for their game against the Rajasthan Royals on April 25. Coetzee, who was unsold at this year's auction , has previously played for Mumbai Indians and Gujarat Titans in the IPL and has picked up 15 wickets in 14 matches. They are currently placed 5th on the points table and will face the Chennai Super Kings later today (April 18). Meanwhile, SRH were also boosted by the arrival of skipper Pat Cummins. Payne played two matches for the franchise this season before being ruled out of the tournament due to an ankle injury. Cricbuzz.com (Cricbuzz Platforms LIMITED) Fleming throws weight behind Ruturaj Gaikwad amidst poor returns SRH vs CSK IPL 2026 | Cricbuzz.com Fleming throws weight behind Ruturaj Gaikwad amidst poor returns Stephen Fleming, the head coach of Chennai Super Kings, came to the defense of his team's captain, Ruturaj Gaikwad, who has been enduring a poor run in the ongoing Indian Premier League. "He's going fine," Fleming assured ahead of CSK's encounter against Sunrisers Hyderabad. With most of SRH's senior pacers unable to trouble opposition batters, the hosts had to hand debuts to two pacers in their previous match against Rajasthan Royals. However, CSK would not want to miss out on cashing on a good start. With Ayush Mhatre also having a couple of good knocks coming into the contest, the opportunity presents itself for CSK to tinker with their opening combination. While the SRH-CSK encounter is also set to take place on the same surface, CSK would hope to ensure that there isn't a repeat performance from the pace duo. Download App Play Store App Store WhatsApp KKR vs RR IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals eye return to winning ways against Kolkata Knight Riders The Royals head into the contest against KKR looking to secure their second away win of the season. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has led the charge with the bat for the Royals this season, piling up 200 runs at a strike rate in excess of 250, while Dhruv Jurel sits third on the scoring charts with 176 runs to his name so far. Kolkata Knight Riders vs Rajasthan Royals IPL 2026 standings With four wins from five games, the Rajasthan Royals are currently third in the IPL 2026 points table with eight points. RR captain Riyan Parag will be hoping for a swift response from his side after suffering their first defeat of the campaign against Sunrisers Hyderabad in their last outing. KKR vs RR in IPL Rajasthan Royals have won 14 of the 32 KKR vs RR IPL head-to-head matches while Kolkata Knight Riders have emerged victorious 16 times. Jofra Archer has spearheaded the pace department with seven scalps, while Nandre Burger and Sandeep Sharma have contributed five wickets each. Meanwhile, Ravi Bishnoi has been the Royals’ go-to player with the ball, having taken nine wickets so far. The Royals kicked off their campaign in commanding fashion, securing home victories against Chennai Super Kings, Mumbai Indians, and Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the ACA Stadium in Guwahati, along with an impressive away win over Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad. Yashasvi Jaiswal is second on RR’s scoring charts with 184 runs and he will enter the contest at the Eden Gardens on Sunday with fond memories of smashing the fastest fifty in IPL off just 13 balls against KKR at the same venue three years back. The KKR vs RR IPL 2026 match on April 19 will start at 3:30 PM Indian Standard Time (IST) and will be available to watch on live streaming and live telecast. This will be the Royals’ first day game of the season.
